title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. heleophyllus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. heleophyllus, also known as water buttercup, is a subspecies of aquatic plants that thrive in shallow water areas, typically found in marshes and ponds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. heleophyllus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and poultices to harness its medicinal benefits, although it's essential to note that the plant can be toxic in large quantities and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
